title: Carrier-assisted One-pot Sample Preparation for Targeted Proteomics Analysis of Small Numbers of Human Cells
author: Kendall Martin

steps.title: Pretreatment of PCR tubes
steps.instruction: Pretreatment of PCR tubes

steps.title: Add 100 µL of nonhuman (e
steps.instruction: Add 100 µL of nonhuman (e.g., Shewanella oneidensis )cell lysate digests at 0.2 µg/µL to PCR tubes. Incubate at room temperature for overnight to coat PCR tube surface.

steps.title: Remove the cell lysate digests by...
steps.instruction: Remove the cell lysate digests by pipetting, rinse PCR tubes with HPLC-grade water for 3 times, and then airdry PCR tubes in a fume hood.

steps.title: Store coated PCR tubes into 4...
steps.instruction: Store coated PCR tubes into 4 °C freezer till further use.

steps.title: FACS sorting
steps.instruction: FACS sorting

steps.title: Align a fluorescence-activated cell sorter (FACS) (e
steps.instruction: Align a fluorescence-activated cell sorter (FACS) (e.g., BD Influx flow cytometer) into a 96-well PCR plate using fluorescent beads with dyes that can be excited by laser source to provide reliable reference signals.

steps.title: Verify that the FACS machine is...
steps.instruction: Verify that the FACS machine is well aimed for sorting cells into the bottom of PCR plates: 1) Set the angle of the sort stream to the minimum possible to maximize the success rate so that the cells will be sorted into the bottoms of wells rather than hit the sides; 2) To aim the machine, use the test stream to ‘sort’ droplets of PBS buffer (e.g., 100 droplets) onto the well of empty plate to make sure that the collected droplets are on the bottom. If the droplets do not land onto the right position, recalibrate the cell sorter and repeat till the droplets in the sort stream are collected correctly.

steps.title: Place the coated PCR tubes into 96-well PCR tube rack
steps.instruction: Place the coated PCR tubes into 96-well PCR tube rack.

steps.title: Sort desired numbers of human breast...
steps.instruction: Sort desired numbers of human breast cells (MCF7 or MCF10A) into precoated PCR tubes.

steps.title: Immediately centrifuge the sorted breast cells...
steps.instruction: Immediately centrifuge the sorted breast cells at 100 x g for 10 min at 4 °C C to keep them at the bottom of the tube to avoid potential cell loss.

steps.title: Store the sorted cells into -80...
steps.instruction: Store the sorted cells into -80 °C freezer until further analysis.

steps.title: Addition of protein carrier, heavy internal standards, and TFE
steps.instruction: Addition of protein carrier, heavy internal standards, and TFE

steps.title: Add 4 µL of 25 mM...
steps.instruction: Add 4 µL of 25 mM NH 4 HCO 3 into PCR tubes containing collected cells.

steps.title: Add 1 µL of 10 ng/µL...
steps.instruction: Add 1 µL of 10 ng/µL bovine serum albumin (BSA) in 25 mM NH 4 HCO 3 into sample tubes.

steps.title: Add 0
steps.instruction: Add 0.3 µL of 100 fmol/µL crude heavy peptide standards (total 30 fmol) into sample tubes. Note: Aliquots of crude heavy peptide standards for EGFR/MAPK pathway proteins are made at a nominal concentration of 100 fmol/µL in 25 mM NH 4 HCO 3 and dispense of the small volume of crude heavy peptide standards at 100 fmol/µL into the middle of the solution and make sure there is no left-over liquid in the pipet tip.

steps.title: Cell lysis and protein denaturation
steps.instruction: Cell lysis and protein denaturation

steps.title: Add 9 µL of 100% TFE...
steps.instruction: Add 9 µL of 100% TFE into sample tubes with the final concentration of ~60% TFE.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 1500 x g for...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 1500 x g for 5 min and then gently vortex at 100 x g for 3 min.

steps.title: Sonicate FACS-sorted breast cells on ice...
steps.instruction: Sonicate FACS-sorted breast cells on ice for 1 min with 1-min interval for a total of 5 cycles at the amplitude at 70% and 0.5 Hz .

steps.title: Incubate samples at 90 °C for...
steps.instruction: Incubate samples at 90 °C for 1 h for protein denaturation using a PCR thermocycler with the heated-lid option. Note: TFE is a volatile solvent with boiling point of 74 °C , and thus the heated-lid option is selected to avoid complete drying with substantial sample loss.

steps.title: Cool samples to room temperature with...
steps.instruction: Cool samples to room temperature with centrifugation at 1500 g for 3 min.

steps.title: Reduction and alkylation (optional)
steps.instruction: Reduction and alkylation (optional)

steps.title: Add 0
steps.instruction: Add 0.6 µL of 50 mM DTT with the final concentration of 2 mM. DTT solution need to be freshly prepared each time. Note: Weigh 77.5 mg of DTT and then dissolve into 1 mL of 25 mM NH 4 HCO 3 to make 500 mM DTT. 10 µL of 500 mM DTT is then added into 90 µL of 25 mM NH 4 HCO 3 to make 50 mM DTT.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 1500 g for 5...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 1500 g for 5 min and then gently mixed at 850 rpm for 3 min.

steps.title: Incubate at 56 °C for 1...
steps.instruction: Incubate at 56 °C for 1 h with gentle shaking at 100 g .

steps.title: Cool samples to room temperature and...
steps.instruction: Cool samples to room temperature and centrifuge at 1500 g for 3 min.

steps.title: Add 0
steps.instruction: Add 0.5 µL of 60 mM IAA to the RT-PCR tube with the final concentration of ~2 mM. IAA solution need to be freshly prepared each time. Note: Weigh 74.3 mg of IAA and then dissolve into 1 mL of 25 mM NH 4 HCO 3 to make 400 mM IAA. 15 µL of 400 mM IAA is added into 85 µL of 25 mM NH 4 HCO 3 to make 60 mM IAA.

steps.title: Incubate in the dark at room...
steps.instruction: Incubate in the dark at room temperature for 30 min with gentle shaking at 100 g . Note: The reduction and alkylation steps are optional when there are no cysteine-containing peptides selected for target proteins.

steps.title: Trypsin digestion
steps.instruction: Trypsin digestion

steps.title: Reduce the sample volume down to...
steps.instruction: Reduce the sample volume down to ~4 µL for greatly reducing TFE volume using a Speed Vac concentrator. Note: TFE is a volatile solvent with boiling point at 74 °C . High percentage TFE inhibits trypsin digestion.

steps.title: Add 9 µL of 25 mM...
steps.instruction: Add 9 µL of 25 mM NH 4 HCO 3 and 1-3 µL of 15 ng/µL trypsin for digestion with the final trypsin concentration 1-3 ng/µL. Note: For 10 FACS-sorted human breast cells (~1 ng), the ratio of trypsin enzyme over protein is ≥15:1, 750-fold higher than that in standard trypsin digestion (the ratio of 1:50).

steps.title: Mix gently at 100 g for...
steps.instruction: Mix gently at 100 g for 3 min and incubate overnight (~16 hours) at 37 °C .

steps.title: Add 0
steps.instruction: Add 0.5 µL of 5% formic acid to stop enzymatic reaction.

steps.title: Centrifuge for 1 h at 1500 g
steps.instruction: Centrifuge for 1 h at 1500 g .

steps.title: Store in -80 °C freezer until...
steps.instruction: Store in -80 °C freezer until further LC-SRM analysis or in 4 °C freezer for immediate analysis.

steps.title: Preparation for direct LC-SRM analysis
steps.instruction: Preparation for direct LC-SRM analysis

steps.title: Remove the cap of PCR tube...
steps.instruction: Remove the cap of PCR tube that is used for collection (Step 2) and processing (Step 6) of FACS-sorted breast cells.

steps.title: Insert PCR tube into LC vial
steps.instruction: Insert PCR tube into LC vial.

steps.title: Close the LC vial for LC-SRM analysis. Note
steps.instruction: Close the LC vial for LC-SRM analysis. Note: For small numbers of cells processed in 96-well PCR plate, the mat cover will be added to seal the plate for direct LC-SRM analysis.

steps.title: LC-SRM analysis
steps.instruction: LC-SRM analysis

steps.title: Analyze samples using UPLC coupled to...
steps.instruction: Analyze samples using UPLC coupled to a triple quadrupole mass spectrometer.

steps.title: Connect capillary C18 columns (75 µm i
steps.instruction: Connect capillary C18 columns (75 µm i.d. × 20 cm for standard gradient and 100 µm i.d. × 10 cm for short gradient) to a chemically etched 20 μm i.d. fused silica electrospray emitter via a stainless metal union.

steps.title: Use 20 µL sample loop for...
steps.instruction: Use 20 µL sample loop for directly loading all of the sample (~15 µL in total) into LC column to maximize detection sensitivity.

steps.title: Use 0
steps.instruction: Use 0.1% formic acid in water as mobile phase A and 0.1% formic acid in 90% acetonitrile as mobile phases B for capillary RPLC separation.

steps.title: Use the binary LC gradient at...
steps.instruction: Use the binary LC gradient at flow rates of 300 nL/min for standard gradient when large numbers of target proteins (>10) are measured. Standard gradient: 5-20% B in 26 min, 20-25% B in 10 min, 25-40% B in 8 min, 40-95% B in 1 min and at 95% B for 7 min for a total of 52 min and the analytical column re-equilibrated at 99.5% A for 8 min.

steps.title: Use the binary LC gradient at...
steps.instruction: Use the binary LC gradient at flow rates of 400 nL/min for short gradient when small numbers of target proteins (≤10) are measured. Short gradient for fast separation: 5-95% B in 5 min and the analytical column re-equilibrated at 99.5% A for 5 min with a total 10 min LC run time.

steps.title: Operate the QQQ mass spectrometer with...
steps.instruction: Operate the QQQ mass spectrometer with ion spray voltages of 2,400 ± 100 V, a capillary offset voltage of 35 V, a skimmer offset voltage of -5 V and a capillary inlet temperature of 220 °C .

steps.title: Obtain the other QQQ MS parameters...
steps.instruction: Obtain the other QQQ MS parameters from automatic tuning and calibration without further optimization.

steps.title: Spike different concentrations of crude heavy...
steps.instruction: Spike different concentrations of crude heavy peptide standards into 20-50 ng/µL of nonhuman cell lysate digests before LC-SRM analysis of samples to determine peptide LC retention time (RT) for building scheduled SRM method and potential interference transitions for accurate quantification.

steps.title: Use the RT scheduled SRM mode...
steps.instruction: Use the RT scheduled SRM mode for multiplexed quantification with the scan window of ≥6 min. Note: The scan window will be adjusted accordingly depending on the number of transitions in a certain time window.

steps.title: Set the total cycle time to...
steps.instruction: Set the total cycle time to 1 s, and the dwell time for each transition is automatically adjusted depending on the number of transitions scanned at different retention time windows. A minimal dwell time 10 ms is needed for each SRM transition. All target proteins can be simultaneously monitored in a single LC-SRM analysis

steps.title: Data Analysis
steps.instruction: Data Analysis

steps.title: Import raw data files into publicly...
steps.instruction: Import raw data files into publicly available Skyline software [33] for data visualization of each target peptide to determine their detectability.

steps.title: Use two criteria to determine SRM...
steps.instruction: Use two criteria to determine SRM peak detection and integration: 1) same LC retention time; 2) approximately the same relative SRM peak intensity ratios across multiple transitions between endogenous (light) peptides and their corresponding (heavy) isotope-labeled peptide internal standards.

steps.title: Inspect all the SRM data manually...
steps.instruction: Inspect all the SRM data manually to ensure correct SRM peak assignment and SRM peak boundary for reliable quantification using the above two criteria.

steps.title: Use the best transition having high...
steps.instruction: Use the best transition having high SRM signal but without matrix interference for protein quantification and the other transitions as a reference.

steps.title: Use multiple replicates to obtain the...
steps.instruction: Use multiple replicates to obtain the standard deviation (SD) and coefficient of variation (CV).

steps.title: Calculate the signal-to-noise ratio (S/N) by...
steps.instruction: Calculate the signal-to-noise ratio (S/N) by the peak apex intensity over the average background noise within a retention time region of ±15 s for standard gradient and ±6 s for short gradient. The limit of detection (LOD) and the limit of quantification (LOQ) are defined as the lowest concentration points at which the S/N ratio is at least 3 and 7, respectively.

steps.title: Apply one additional criteria for conservatively...
steps.instruction: Apply one additional criteria for conservatively determining the LOQ values besides S/N ≥7: surrogate peptide response against different cell numbers must be within the linear dynamic range.

steps.title: Use the Excel software to plot all calibration curves
steps.instruction: Use the Excel software to plot all calibration curves. Load the RAW data from QQQ MS into publicly available Skyline software to display graphs of extracted ion chromatograms (XICs) of multiple transitions of target proteins monitored.

steps.title: after FACS sorting, immediate centrifugation is...
steps.instruction: after FACS sorting, immediate centrifugation is required to ensure that the collected cells are at the bottom of tubes or wells because low volume (~15 µL) is used to process small numbers of cells;

steps.title: prior to the addition of trypsin...
steps.instruction: prior to the addition of trypsin for digestion, the TFE levels need to be reduced down to ≤10% for effective digestion. To avoid drying out samples resulting in unrecoverable loss, frequently checking the sample volume (~4 µL of the remaining volume) is suggested during Speed Vac concentrating. One-pot sample preparation can be further simplified by removal of reduction and alkylation steps with neglectable effect on digestion efficiency. One drawback is that cysteine containing peptides cannot be selected as surrogate peptides for target proteins. To avoid Speed Vac concentrating step for TFE removal we consider replacing TFE with MS-compatible surfactants (e.g., DDM: n-Dodecyl β-D-maltoside) for cell lysis because frequent checking sample volume severely prevents automation of sample processing with low throughput. This was evidenced by our recent data for DDM-assisted global single-cell proteomics analysis (unpublished). In addition, single carrier protein (e.g., BSA) may not be sufficient to unbiasedly reduce surface adsorption losses for every protein. This was evidenced by a few undetected proteins at >30,000 copies per cell (e.g., MAP2K

steps.title: in 10 MCF7 cells that should have been detected [30]
steps.instruction: in 10 MCF7 cells that should have been detected [30]. Non-human cell lysates presumably can be used as a more effective proteome carrier in contrast to our current BSA carrier because there are many different types of nonhuman proteins (e.g., >3000 proteins for Shewanella oneidensis ) with less interference for SRM detection of human proteins. We are working on several different ways to further improve cLC-SRM performance with the potential of moving towards sensitive targeted single-cell proteomics. When compared to other MS-based single-cell proteomics methods that require specific devices, cLC-SRM can be readily implemented in any MS proteomics laboratory without additional investment on specific devices for sample processing. But it can only be used for quantitative analysis of target proteins of interest. Unlike global single-cell proteomics methods for relative untargeted quantification, cLC-SRM can provide accurate or absolute quantification of target proteins in small numbers of cells including single cells. Furthermore, cLC-SRM has significant advantages over conventional targeted single-cell proteomics using antibody-based immunoassays in terms of multiplexing and quantitation specificity and accuracy. Future developments will focus on significant improvements in detection sensitivity and sample throughput for enabling rapid absolute quantification of most proteins (e.g., important negative feedback regulators) in single human cells. Automation of cLC-SRM is another direction to improve its robustness, reproducibility, and the overall sample throughput with commercially available liquid handlers. We anticipate that cLC-SRM will be broadly used for multiplexed accurate quantification of target proteins in small subpopulations of cells and rare tumor cells as well as mass-limited samples. In turn, it will be greatly beneficial to current biomedical research and systems biology.
